req.title: Schedule performance review — variant 5
req.statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) scheduled_at >= now+7d (strict lower bound), (2) 2FA confirmation before scheduling succeeds, (3) 422 on sub-7-day or past dates. I derive: EP positive partition (date > now+7d, 2FA supplied), BVA lower boundary (exactly now+7d = valid; now+7d-1s = invalid), NEG for past date, DT for 2FA absent + valid date combo, STC for the state machine: unauthenticated -> 2FA-pending -> scheduled. Five tests cover all mandatory case types and all stated error pathways.

tests.name: test_schedule_review_valid_date_with_2fa_succeeds
preconditions: scheduled_at = now + 10 days (well inside valid partition)
expected: Notification record created for both manager and reviewee
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_valid_date_with_2fa_succeeds(
    manager_api_client,
    reviewee_id,
    complete_2fa,
    assert_notification_sent,
):
    scheduled_at = (
        datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=10)
    ).isoformat()

    complete_2fa(manager_api_client)

    resp = manager_api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json={"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "review_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "scheduled"
    assert_notification_sent(body["review_id"], roles=["manager", "reviewee"])

tests.name: test_schedule_review_past_date_returns_422
preconditions: scheduled_at = now - 1 day (past date, clearly invalid partition)
expected: Response error code indicates date_too_early or equivalent
No review record created in database
trace: R-WEB-096: past dates shall be rejected
R-WEB-096: error pathway 422 when scheduled_at < now+7d
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_past_date_returns_422(
    manager_api_client,
    reviewee_id,
    complete_2fa,
    review_db_count,
):
    scheduled_at = (
        datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=1)
    ).isoformat()

    complete_2fa(manager_api_client)
    count_before = review_db_count()

    resp = manager_api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json={"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "scheduled_at" in str(body.get("detail", "")).lower() or \
           body.get("error_code") in ("date_too_early", "invalid_date")
    assert review_db_count() == count_before

tests.name: test_schedule_review_exactly_7_days_ahead_is_accepted
preconditions: scheduled_at = now + exactly 7 days (lower boundary of valid range)
expected: Boundary value accepted — confirms inclusive boundary
trace: R-WEB-096: at least 7 days in advance — boundary value now+7d must be valid
R-WEB-096: BVA lower bound ON point
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_exactly_7_days_ahead_is_accepted(
    manager_api_client,
    reviewee_id,
    complete_2fa,
    freeze_time,
):
    with freeze_time() as frozen_now:
        scheduled_at = (
            frozen_now + timedelta(days=7)
        ).isoformat()

        complete_2fa(manager_api_client)

        resp = manager_api_client.post(
            "/performance-reviews/schedule",
            json={"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at},
        )

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    assert resp.json()["status"] == "scheduled"

tests.name: test_schedule_review_6_days_23_hours_ahead_returns_422
preconditions: scheduled_at = now + 7 days - 1 second (one second below valid boundary)
expected: Sub-7-day boundary correctly rejected — confirms exclusive lower bound
trace: R-WEB-096: sub-7-day dates shall be rejected
R-WEB-096: BVA lower bound OFF-by-one point
R-WEB-096: error pathway 422 when scheduled_at < now+7d
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_6_days_23_hours_ahead_returns_422(
    manager_api_client,
    reviewee_id,
    complete_2fa,
    freeze_time,
):
    with freeze_time() as frozen_now:
        scheduled_at = (
            frozen_now + timedelta(days=7) - timedelta(seconds=1)
        ).isoformat()

        complete_2fa(manager_api_client)

        resp = manager_api_client.post(
            "/performance-reviews/schedule",
            json={"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at},
        )

    assert resp.status_code == 422

tests.name: test_schedule_review_without_2fa_is_rejected
preconditions: User authenticated as manager (session token only, no 2FA token)
trace: R-WEB-096: action requires two-factor authentication confirmation
R-WEB-096: DT — valid date BUT missing 2FA => must fail
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_without_2fa_is_rejected(
    manager_api_client,
    reviewee_id,
    review_db_count,
):
    scheduled_at = (
        datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=10)
    ).isoformat()

    count_before = review_db_count()

    # Deliberately skip complete_2fa — raw manager session only
    resp = manager_api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json={"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at},
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (401, 403)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "2fa" in str(body).lower() or "two_factor" in str(body).lower() \
           or "mfa" in str(body).lower()
    assert review_db_count() == count_before

tests.name: test_schedule_review_state_transition_unauthenticated_to_scheduled
actions: POST /auth/login with manager credentials (state: unauthenticated -> authenticated)
expected: Each state transition returns expected HTTP 2xx
Full state path exercised without shortcut
trace: R-WEB-096: 2FA confirmation — full state machine traversal
R-WEB-096: review scheduled outcome after complete auth lifecycle
STC: covers all transitions from entry to terminal scheduled state
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_state_transition_unauthenticated_to_scheduled(
    unauthenticated_client,
    manager_credentials,
    reviewee_id,
    otp_for_manager,
):
    scheduled_at = (
        datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=14)
    ).isoformat()

    r1 = unauthenticated_client.post(
        "/auth/login", json=manager_credentials
    )
    assert r1.status_code == 200
    session_token = r1.json()["session_token"]

    unauthenticated_client.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {session_token}"

    r2 = unauthenticated_client.post("/auth/2fa/initiate")
    assert r2.status_code == 200

    r3 = unauthenticated_client.post(
        "/auth/2fa/verify", json={"otp": otp_for_manager()}
    )
    assert r3.status_code == 200

    r4 = unauthenticated_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json={"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at},
    )
    assert r4.status_code == 201
    assert r4.json()["status"] == "scheduled"
